subject: LU-2771 dlmlock: compress out unused space

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Rosso Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 1:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/399e61d2-725c-11e2-aad1-52540035b04c).  Ran 1 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 1: (5 inline comments)

This seems like a decent change. 

The thing I am wondering about is should the end API for "locks" be macrotized.  The bitwize operations are transparent but in some spots they can be quite lengthy.  It seems like only a handful of Macros would be needed (My names might not be the best just trying to show what I am wondering about).  

I seems there is some review context outside of this ticket was it agreed to move to bitwise operations?

Patch Set 1: (4 inline comments)

I agree with Keith's basic idea, but I really think it is out of scope here.

Patch Set 1:

P.S. There was one out-of-band question I had of Oleg and Andreas that has still to be answered:

That still leaves a question about implementation.  Strictly speaking, with the
bit fields gone, there is no compelling reason to rewrite the
definitions for the bits, except that the stuff I spun out of a code generator
is a lot cleaner than the original.

That leaves three choices on the implementation:

 1. Minimize the patch (go back to the uglier code :)
 2. Incorporate the generated defines into the lustre_dlm.h header (as done)
 3. replace the defines in lustre_dlm.h with a #include of the generated text

The advantage of the third is that all that wireshark stuff can more
readily use it and I can spit out stuff usable in crash for displaying
comprehensible bits that do not require you to go cross-eyed.  e.g.:

 > crash> ldlm_lock_flags ~0xFF
 > REPLAY              INTENT_ONLY         HAS_INTENT          DISCARD_DATA
 > NO_TIMEOUT          BLOCK_NOWAIT        TEST_LOCK           CANCEL_ON_BLOCK
 > DENY_ON_CONTENTION  AST_DISCARD_DATA    FAIL_LOC            SKIPPED
 > CBPENDING           WAIT_NOREPROC       CANCEL              LOCAL_ONLY
 > FAILED              CANCELING           LOCAL               LVB_READY
 > KMS_IGNORE          CP_REQD             CLEANED             ATOMIC_CB
 > BL_AST              BL_DONE             NO_LRU              FAIL_NOTIFIED
 > DESTROYED           SERVER_LOCK         RES_LOCKED          WAITED
 > NS_SRV

So, pick one and I'll do a 13th patch, this time without the "test"
attribute. ;)

Patch Set 1:

P.P.S.  ``I seems there is some review context outside of this ticket was it agreed to move to bitwise operations?''

I tried overlaying bit fields and mask bits.  It doesn't work.  GCC insists upon its right to place bit fields at will, rendering it impossible to overlay anonymous unions and bit field structures.  Each platform and GCC version would/could be different.  That is too unwieldy to code up.  Also, anonymous structures are only in the very most recent GCCs.

Summary:  to combine the bit fields into the mask word required going to a mask.

Patch Set 1:

If you already have a version that cleans up the code, you may as well use that one, even if it changes more lines.

Patch Set 1:

Hi Andreas,

``If you already have a version that cleans up the code''

I actually don't have a patch that leaves the unchanged bit defines alone, so for alternative #1, I'd have work to do.  To create the patch as it is, I emit the new defines for lustre_dlm.h and then replace the text in that file by hand.  It would be easier for me to remove the text and insert a #include instead.  With that alternative, I would add four files:  lustre_dlm_flags.def, luster_dlm_flags.tpl, lustre_dlm_flags.h and lustre_dlm_flags.c.  The first two are hand edited, the second two are generated and the last is for creating a crash extension (not part of lustre itself).

To derive the generated text, "apt get autogen" (or similar).  The derived files should still be incorporated into the GIT repository.

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

See comments on http://review.whamcloud.com/5288 (which I thought was this patch).

I'm fine with the #3 option, but I'd prefer if there were some commonly-available tool to generate the header, so it could be built by default on any system, instead of only occasionally.

Patch Set 1: (1 inline comment)

``I'm fine with the #3 option, but I'd prefer if there were some commonly-available tool to generate the header, so it could be built by default on any system, instead of only occasionally.''

autogen _is_ commonly available.  All popular/common distributions have it by asking for it via "yum" or "zypper" or whatever.  It is used by gcc, gnutls, gdb and a number of other projects.

Uploaded patch set 2.

Actually, someone working on the SLES11 SP2 patches submitted about a hundred patches, and it has clogged up our builder nodes...  Resubmitting the patch several times won't speed things up.  You can check build.whamcloud.com for status.

Patch Set 4:

Patch Set 8:

I'm going to guess that a "recovery-small" timeout failure is known, since it is unlikely that respelling bit flags is going to have that kind of effect.

I'll open a lower priority bug to rewrite the bit manipulations as macro invocations, but it won't cover them all.  There is at least one place where the address of a __u64 type gets passed through multiple layers of function calls before the bits are fiddled.  I do not know if that argument can readily be replaced with a lock pointer (which would enable the use of the macros).

parents.subject: LU-2392 kerberos: GSS keyring is broken >=2.6.29

commit.subject: LU-2771 dlmlock: compress out unused space
commit.message:
LU-2771 dlmlock: compress out unused space

* lustre/include/lustre_dlm.h: Remove all bit fields and the unused
  weighing callback procedure.  respell LDLM_AST_DISCARD_DATA as
  LDLM_FL_AST_DISCARD_DATA to match other flags.
* .gitignore: ignore emacs temporary files
* autogen.sh: rebuild the lock bits, if autogen is available.
* contrib/bit-masks/lustre_dlm_flags.def: define the ldlm_lock flags
* contrib/bit-masks/lustre_dlm_flags.tpl: template for emitting text
* contrib/bit-masks/Makefile: construct the .c and .h files
  The .c file is for constructing a crash extension and is not
  preserved.
* contrib/bit-masks/.gitignore: ignore built products
* lustre/contrib/wireshark/packet-lustre.c: use built files instead
  of local versions of the defines.

In the rest of the modified sources, replace flag field references
with bit mask references.

* lustre/osc/osc_lock.c: removed osc_lock_weigh, too

Signed-off-by: Bruce Korb <bruce_korb@xyratex.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Keith Mannthey <Keith.Mannthey@intel.com>
Change-Id: I104522e4012deb0dd8b9d12c2d1f3fd3851708cb
